sections.about: This metric captures treated or untreated discharge to rivers, streams or other surface-water bodies under BRSR Principle 6 Essential Indicator water discharge by destination. Lower discharge is generally better where the strategic objective is recycling and ZLD. A lower value indicates better performance.
sections.performanceAndDrivers: JSW and Jindal report zero surface water discharge across all three years. SAIL discharges 22.36 million kL in FY2023, worsening to 22.99 million kL in FY2024 and 41.81 million kL in FY2025, despite noting P/S/T treatment; ZLD targets have not yet translated into low reported discharge. Tata discharges 12.28 million kL in FY2023, worsening to 13.85 million kL in FY2024 before improving to 12.00 million kL in FY2025.
sections.tataPositioning: In FY2025, Tata ranks 3rd with 12.00 million kL; JSW and Jindal are best at zero. Tata ranks 3rd across all three years, below SAIL in FY2023 and FY2024, and above SAIL in FY2025 as SAIL's discharge rises sharply. Tata's FY2024-25 trend improves but rank is unchanged.
sections.targets: Tata’s BRSR identifies ZED from Works premises and reuse of treated effluent in steelmaking processes such as coke quenching, BF slag granulation, gas cleaning, slag quenching and dust suppression. SAIL’s ZLD goal and upcoming-unit ZLD concept are relevant.
sections.comparabilityNotes: Treatment level matters. SAIL's FY2025 discharge is reported with P/S/T treatment, so environmental impact is not equivalent to untreated discharge. Jindal FY2022-23 surface water discharge was not explicitly reported and is treated as zero for this indicator. Tata Steel FY2022-23 water discharge metrics were not explicitly reported on a purely standalone basis but rather collectively for Tata Steel India and its Indian subsidiaries.
